title: Relationship Mistakes You Can't Come Back From
text: there are three huge mistakes in relationships I've personally made that you just can't come back from over time though those mistakes have completely changed my perspective on relationships and as I continued to learn from them and then met my wife their lessons became even more relevant so let's talk about them mistake one I prioritize someone who didn't prioritize me have you ever been friends with a girl realized how cool you thought she was and then started to develop what seemed like one-sided feelings for her because I have to there was a girl in my high school who we can call Tina and I was really into her so what did I do about it contrary to my Norm at the time I actually worked up the courage to tell her so what happened next it flopped and I was extremely disappointed and I took it personally as if there was something wrong with me as a person but I cared about her so we remained friends I couldn't shake my feelings though so over time I told her again and she still didn't respond the way that I had hoped is there any chance this hits close to home here's how I took it pretty quickly I started to believe that her feelings towards me defined me over time I dwell on the rejections I couldn't sh shake the feeling that if I wasn't worth it now I really didn't know how it was going to be the valuable lesson this taught me in time was this I was much better suited spending energy chasing someone who valued me and cared about me intimately as opposed to someone who clearly didn't from the start and hearing that you might think well yeah obviously or maybe you're thinking damn either way sometimes it's just that simple but it can be so hard to see in the moment I mean look at me she didn't match my feelings for a pretty large sample size I wasted a lot of time chasing someone who had no int of ever chasing me and it's way too easy to dwell on that failure that feeling of not being enough like it defines you but the sooner you can dislodge that lie the sooner you can give your time to a person who will value and care about you mistake two I thought I could fix everything I've shared a bit about this story before but it's such an important warning sign that I wanted to share it in more detail in

title: Relationship Mistakes You Can't Come Back From
text: you can give your time to a person who will value and care about you mistake two I thought I could fix everything I've shared a bit about this story before but it's such an important warning sign that I wanted to share it in more detail in college I dated a girl and we'll call her Sally that had some pretty serious personal trauma this was made clear to me by her in a healthy way early on but I didn't realize how deep it ran for her and I also didn't realize how ILO ipped I would end up being to handle those things when tensions were heightened Sally dealt with severe anxiety and insecurity and she would pretty drastically shift between needing time and space and needing to Lean on Me heavily sometimes she would completely shut down and this was pretty confusing to me I thought that since we were Partners I should be able to fix all of this but I couldn't seem to eventually that confused me more my confusion turned to hurt and my hurt didn't make things better it made things worse at the end of the day I realized that walking further into a broken relationship thinking that I have the tools to fix it when I actually don't is a recipe for disappointment this isn't to say you shouldn't fight to overcome issues in healthy relationships because you need to but this was not an example of a normal issue and I wasn't mature enough at the time to cultivate a healthy relationship we both had our things to work on but me thinking that I and I alone was responsible for fixing everything is what sunk the ship in the end mistake three I was with someone for the wrong reasons ask yourself this what is it that you're truthfully after in a relationship sometimes we can convince ourselves that we're dating for the right reasons but we aren't later in college I met met a girl through a mutual friend and we got along well right away we can call her Jill our initial mistake was making our relationship official way too fast and there's a reason for that this was a time in my life in which I needed to do some pretty serious self-reflection looking back I was after all the wrong things when it came to relationships if I was brutally honest I didn't really want a connection with a partner I wanted physicality and praise for myself in place of investing in myself though I invested in this shallow relationship and falsified that connection with Jill because she gave me what I thought I wanted at a more convenient price eventually she broke up with me and this

title: Relationship Mistakes You Can't Come Back From
text: or myself in place of investing in myself though I invested in this shallow relationship and falsified that connection with Jill because she gave me what I thought I wanted at a more convenient price eventually she broke up with me and this crutch that I used to prop up my self-esteem and desires was just snatched away in a way you could say I was heartbroken but after reality set in I just felt disappointed in myself I wasn't worried about a connection here I was worried about a transaction I learned that unless you're with someone for the right reasons and feel steady in your head space then you won't be able to sustain a healthy relationship you can't possibly expect another person to solely carry your needs and wants good or bad because over time they're going to crumble under that responsibility if you've made mistakes like like these recently and you feel kind of defeated then here's the good news I wouldn't be the man that I am today without these mistakes learning to prioritize someone who prioriti as you learning that you can't fix everything yourself and learning that you shouldn't chase a relationship unless you're in it for the right reasons these were all painful as I was learning though but I didn't forget about them those mistakes that you make don't Define you you use them to become better the mistakes made transform into Lessons Learned and they help me not only find my wife but become a worthy partner in the process so yes these mistakes might be a death s you can learn from my mistakes and try to avoid them but if you're anything like me other mistakes will keep coming and that's life if you can take the hits learn from them and then be better next time then don't worry you're doing exactly what you're supposed to be doing eventually those mistakes and lessons will be absolutely worth it
